Document:
# Crafting System
[textlink="View Full Wiki Page" link="https://ss14.misfitsystems.net/wiki/index.php/Crafting%20System"/]
## Blueprints & Crafting
Blueprint crafting is the workbench-based side of the Wasteland crafting system. If you have the right blueprint, bench, resources, and tools, you can turn scrap and rare parts into faction gear, armor, and weapons.
[bold]Component[/bold] [bold]Details[/bold] [bold]Blueprints[/bold] Define what you can make and what each recipe needs. Found by exploring the Wasteland, looting enemies, or trading with faction vendors. [bold]Workbenches[/bold] Determine where a recipe can be completed. The Weapon Bench is the most common - others are faction-specific. [bold]Resources[/bold] Raw materials, scrap parts, or intermediate crafted components consumed by the recipe. [bold]Tools[/bold] Required by many recipes but are [bold]not consumed[/bold] during crafting. Keep a screwdriver, welder, and wrench on hand.
[bold]Faction-locked blueprints:[/bold] Some blueprints are restricted to certain factions. Access depends on who you are and which bench you can reach.
Find or buy a blueprint.
Gather all listed resources.
Take everything to the correct workbench.
Open the crafting interface and select the blueprint.
Confirm the recipe - the crafted item will appear in your inventory or next to the bench.
Power armor restoration and Brotherhood field gear. Usually recovered from patrols, bunkers, and chapter stockpiles.
[bold]Blueprint[/bold] [bold]Type[/bold] [bold]T-45 Power Armor Blueprint[/bold] Power Armor [bold]T-60 Tesla Armor Blueprint[/bold] Power Armor [bold]BoS Initiate Armor Blueprint[/bold] Combat Armor [bold]Midwest Brotherhood Armor Blueprint[/bold] Combat Armor [bold]Midwest Brotherhood Armor MK2 Blueprint[/bold] Combat Armor
NCR armor patterns and restored military equipment. Most commonly found in camps, depots, and quartermaster caches.
[bold]Blueprint[/bold] [bold]Type[/bold] [bold]T-45 NCR Power Armor Blueprint[/bold] Power Armor [bold]T-51 NCR Power Armor Blueprint[/bold] Power Armor [bold]T-60 NCR Power Armor Blueprint[/bold] Power Armor [bold]Raider Power Armor Blueprint[/bold] Power Armor [bold]Advanced NCR Power Armor Blueprint[/bold] Power Armor [bold]NCR Desert Vest Blueprint[/bold] Light Armor [bold]NCR Combat Armor Blueprint[/bold] Combat Armor [bold]NCR Combat Armor MK2 Blueprint[/bold] Combat Armor
Legion armor blueprints recovered from outposts, patrol leaders, and veteran warriors.
[bold]Blueprint[/bold] [bold]Type[/bold] [bold]Legionnaire Armor Blueprint[/bold] Combat Armor [bold]Veteran Legionnaire Armor Blueprint[/bold] Combat Armor
Handcrafted tribal weapons and armor. Usually traded by tribal groups or found in remote camps.
[bold]Blueprint[/bold] [bold]Type[/bold] [bold]Handcannon Blueprint[/bold] Revolver [bold]Tribal Handgun Blueprint[/bold] Revolver [bold]Tribal Rifle Blueprint[/bold] Sniper Rifle [bold]Tribal Assault Rifle Blueprint[/bold] Rifle [bold]Tribal Combat Armor Blueprint[/bold] Combat Armor
Faction-neutral blueprints available to any capable crafter with the right bench and parts.
[bold]Blueprint[/bold] [bold]Type[/bold] [bold]Laser Revolver Blueprint[/bold] Energy Pistol [bold]Makeshift Laser Rifle Blueprint[/bold] Energy Rifle [bold]Pipe Rifle (.556) Blueprint[/bold] Rifle [bold]Combat Armor Blueprint[/bold] Combat Armor [bold]Combat Armor MK2 Blueprint[/bold] Combat Armor
